behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename semantics plus CLI execution: the behavior depends on how the tool reads actual repository change data and maps rename records. A mock parser can miss real failures from git command flags/output format or rename detection behavior. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: using a real repo and running the CLI (often via subprocess) is needed to catch those failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text mentions only a Python CLI over git history, with no AI/ML call and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit baseline, rename a file, run the CLI against the repo, and assert report shows the file only under renamed and not under added/deleted.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This failure commonly appears at real subprocess/encoding boundaries when reading git diff output containing binary markers or non-text payloads. In-process stubs can hide decode/crash behavior and git-specific binary diff formatting. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE both point to System. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model dependency or multi-step agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temporary git repo, add and change a binary file, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file changes are reflected in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is handling real git 'unborn HEAD/no commits' repository state, which is exposed through actual git command behavior and exit/status handling. A fake repo adapter may not reproduce exact error modes. REAL-INFRA indicates System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is pure CLI/git handling with no AI/ML dependency and no chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert successful execution with an empty structured report instead of an error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against actual repository history, requiring real git state transitions and CLI execution to catch mismatches from history traversal/diff invocation. Mocks risk tautological passing if expected data is pre-shaped. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE support System. Rejected higher levels: no model-output quality or agent pipeline in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI, and assert reported category counts equal independently computed expected counts from the repo history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on true git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/filesystem + git-state interactions and often surfaced through CLI/subprocess calls. In-process doubles can miss real staging edge cases. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE indicate System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not involve AI/ML outputs or multi-step agentic orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, make the same file edits in two scenarios (staged and unstaged), run the CLI in each scenario, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
